intro:
Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information about the plant Neo-urbania disticha. However, based on the name, it seems to be a combination of the names Neo-urbania and Disticha, which suggests it might be a plant with a similar Latin name to Distichlis spicata (also known as Saltwort). 

Here is a paragraph about Distichlis spicata:

Distichlis spicata, also known as Saltwort or Neo-urbania disticha, is a type of salt-tolerant grass that is commonly found in coastal areas. The health benefits of Distichlis spicata include its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its bioactive constituents. The bioactive constituents of Distichlis spicata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-hypertensive properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Distichlis spicata include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support cardiovascular health and reduce oxidative stress.
